parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Alfentanil — low volume of distribution and rapid offset'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'Alfentanil is useful in short day-case anaesthesia because it has a rapid onset and relatively rapid offset after brief administration.'}

{"topic": "Primary Frca Sample Sbas", "exam": "primary", "curriculum_node_code": null, "model": "openai:gpt-5.5", "generated_at": "2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172", "questions": [{"stem": "A patient undergoes day-case removal of a sebaceous cyst under total intravenous general anaesthesia using propofol and alfentanil. The surgeon infiltrates local anaesthetic at the end of surgery.", "lead": "Which pharmacokinetic property is the best reason for choosing alfentanil rather than fentanyl in this setting?", "options": [{"text": "Clearance", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Alfentanil has a lower clearance than fentanyl, so clearance does not explain its faster offset in this context."}, {"text": "Inactive metabolites", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Inactive metabolites may be advantageous for some drugs, but they are not the main reason alfentanil has a faster offset than fentanyl after brief use."}, {"text": "Potency", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Potency relates to the dose needed to produce an effect and may influence onset, but it is not the best explanation for alfentanil's faster offset."}, {"text": "Volume of distribution", "is_correct": true, "explanation": "Alfentanil has a lower volume of distribution than fentanyl, so after short infusions or boluses it redistributes less extensively and has a faster offset."}, {"text": "pKa", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Alfentanil's low pKa contributes to rapid onset, but the question concerns choosing it for day-case offset rather than onset."}], "explanation": "Alfentanil is useful in short day-case anaesthesia because it has a rapid onset and relatively rapid offset after brief administration. Compared with fentanyl, the lower volume of distribution is the best explanation for its faster offset despite lower clearance.", "curriculum_node_codes": ["1_RA", "1_GA_H_2", "1_GA_B_17", "1_GA_S", "1_GA"], "curriculum_node_scores": [0.7153659755464883, 0.7001266589431907, 0.6884818939513195, 0.6808970545373381, 0.6588554724028182], "title": "Alfentanil — low volume of distribution and rapid offset"}]}
